title: ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. ambiguum
intro: Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. ambiguum, also known as the adder's tongue fern, is a sub-species of the common adder's tongue fern, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Ophioglossaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. ambiguum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional before consumption.
